title: AuthProvider::auth_header: decouple from http::HeaderName/HeaderValue
description:
## Why

AuthProvider::auth_header returns Option<(HeaderName, HeaderValue)> where HeaderName and HeaderValue are from the http crate (re-exported by reqwest). Any third-party implementation of AuthProvider must take on the http crate as a dependency to satisfy the trait signature, defeating the injectable intent. The coupling also makes the trait awkward to mock in tests without reqwest.

## What needs to be done

Change the return type to Option<(String, String)> representing (header-name, header-value). Inside JmapChatClient::call and fetch_session, convert with HeaderName::from_str and HeaderValue::from_str; these can panic/unwrap if the AuthProvider is expected to pre-validate. Update NoneAuth, BearerAuth, BasicAuth impls accordingly. Add a test verifying that a mock AuthProvider returning (String, String) is accepted by the client.

Note: the pre-computation optimization (HeaderValue stored at construction time, not recomputed per-request) still works with this change -- the stored field type changes from HeaderValue to String (or bytes), and the conversion happens once in a wrapper type. Alternatively, keep the internal pre-computation and expose a different public-facing conversion layer.
